display.purpose: Hero headlines only. 'Where are you heading next?' Lives on cinematic photo backgrounds.

headline.purpose: Section titles, destination names on cards, page-level anchors.

title.purpose: Card headings, subsection titles, modal headers.

body.purpose: All running copy. 65-75ch max line length.

label.purpose: ALL CAPS data field names: VISA, SCORE, BUDGET FROM. Section eyebrows, category tags.

shadows.name: flush
shadows.purpose: Barely-there separation — chips at rest, subtle dividers.

shadows.name: elevated
shadows.purpose: Hovered or focused card — warm amber diffuse layer.

shadows.name: cinematic
shadows.purpose: Featured hero moments — amber ring glows the subject.

shadows.name: amber-glow
shadows.purpose: Atmospheric bloom for CTA hover, active passport gateway.

motion.name: quick
motion.purpose: State changes — button hover, chip selection, border-color.

motion.name: dramatic
motion.purpose: Passport gateway zoom transition, full-page transitions.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.purpose: Mobile-to-desktop split — hero image, two-column layout, glass controls card.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.purpose: Wide desktop — page container widens to 1080px, hero image repositions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main CTA — amber fill, pill shape, white text. Used for passport entry, confirmation, primary actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #d97706; color: #ffffff; padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 9999px; border: none; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.19,1,0.22,1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.19,1,0.22,1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.19,1,0.22,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #b45309; transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 0 24px -4px rgba(217,119,6,0.22), 0 0 64px -8px rgba(217,119,6,0.10); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d97706; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action — transparent background, amber text, pill shape. Used in glass-card contexts.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #d97706; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid rgba(217,119,6,0.3); font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.19,1,0.22,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(217,119,6,0.08); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d97706; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Profile Chip (unselected)
components.description: Travel style or passport selector pill — white at rest, amber fill when active.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 6px 14px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1.5px solid #c7bdb2; background: #ffffff; color: #1c1917; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 400; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms ease, color 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(28,25,23,0.05); } .ds-chip:hover { border-color: #d97706; background: #fffbeb; } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d97706; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Profile Chip (active)
components.description: Active travel style chip — amber fill stamps the selection.
components.css: .ds-chip--active { background: #d97706; color: #ffffff; border-color: #d97706; font-weight: 500; } .ds-chip--active:hover { background: #b45309; border-color: #b45309; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Data label badge — amber tint for budget/score highlights.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #fef3c7; color: #92400e; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; }

components.name: Content Card
components.description: Base content surface — warm gradient, paper grain, amber border that deepens on hover.
components.css: .ds-card { background: linear-gradient(160deg, #ffffff 0%, #fef9f3 60%, #fdfaf6 100%); border: 1px solid rgba(180,130,65,0.2); border-radius: 16px; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(28,25,23,0.07), 0 4px 16px -4px rgba(28,25,23,0.07); position: relative; overflow: hidden; transition: box-shadow 180ms ease, transform 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease; } .ds-card::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: url('/assets/ui/editorial-paper-texture.webp') center / cover; border-radius: inherit; opacity: 0.038; pointer-events: none; mix-blend-mode: multiply; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(28,25,23,0.10), 0 12px 32px -6px rgba(28,25,23,0.13), 0 20px 48px -12px rgba(120,75,15,0.10); transform: translateY(-4px); border-color: rgba(217,119,6,0.38); } .ds-card-body { color: #57534e; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; }

components.name: Section Label
components.description: Editorial chapter marker — ALL CAPS, muted, with gradient rule extending right. Used above every major section.
components.css: .ds-section-label { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #a8a29e; margin: 0; } .ds-section-label::after { content: ''; flex: 1; height: 1px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #e7e0d6 0%, transparent 100%); }

components.name: Section Heading
components.description: Data subsection title — 3px amber left border, gradient amber background wash. Used inside Ready pages.
components.html: Visa Requirements
components.css: .ds-section-heading { font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1c1917; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 14px; padding: 5px 14px 5px 12px; border-left: 3px solid #d97706; letter-spacing: -0.02em; background: linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(254,243,199,0.85) 0%, rgba(254,243,199,0.25) 55%, transparent 100%); border-radius: 0 6px 6px 0; }

narrative.overview:
NomadReady's visual system is built around a single unifying metaphor: a physical travel artifact rendered in software. Think of a leather-bound atlas that has been carried through a dozen countries — embossed covers, gold ink worn smooth, paper grain warmed by the sun, edges soft from use. Every surface, every shadow, every typographic choice should feel like it could exist as a printed object you'd find at the bottom of a well-traveled bag.

The system lives in a warm, atmospheric light register — not the sterile white of a SaaS dashboard, not the dark drama of a fintech product, but something closer to afternoon light in a field guide. The dominant palette is Parchment Mist and warm ink tones, punctuated by a single amber accent (Ember Gold) that appears sparingly — used the way a cartographer would use red ink: to mark what matters.

This system explicitly rejects: generic travel SaaS aesthetics, fintech/crypto UI (glassmorphism, neon), childish app design (pastel blobs, confetti, bouncy motion), flat corporate UI, and startup motion language.
keyCharacteristics: Single warm typeface family (Geist Sans) throughout — no serif display, no decorative pairing
Amber accent used at ≤15% surface coverage; its scarcity is intentional
Paper grain texture at 3–7% opacity on all card surfaces via multiply blend
Shadow system has five named tiers; cards lift on hover with warm-tinted shadows
Motion is restrained: state changes at 150–260ms, reveals at 700ms cinematic ease
Hero sections use full-bleed photography with directional dark overlays — text always reads at WCAG AA
Passport gateway experience: cinematic dark (#0c1020) with amber atmospheric glows

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Ember Gold appears on ≤15% of any given screen. Every additional use must earn its place. If you're wondering whether to use it, the answer is probably no.

rules.name: The No-Cool-Gray Rule
rules.body: Every neutral must be warm — nudged toward red/amber. #57534e not #6b7280. If a gray looks bluish or neutral in context, replace it.

rules.name: The Night Register Rule
rules.body: #0c1020 (Cinematic Night) is reserved exclusively for full-immersion surfaces: the passport gateway, full-bleed hero overlays, modal backdrops. Never for cards, sidebars, or utility surfaces.

rules.name: The Tight Display Rule
rules.body: Display-size text always sets at −0.04em or tighter. Loose tracking on large headings reads as amateur. The atlas is authoritative.

rules.name: The Label Uppercase Rule
rules.body: Labels above data fields are always ALL CAPS, 0.7rem, 700 weight, tracking +0.08em, color ink-muted. Breaking it anywhere breaks it everywhere.

rules.name: The Warm Shadow Rule
rules.body: No neutral or cool shadows, ever. Every shadow uses rgba(28,25,23,...) (warm ink) or rgba(120,75,15,...) (amber ink). If a shadow looks gray, it is wrong.

rules.name: The Lift Rule
rules.body: Cards at rest use Card shadow. On hover, Elevated shadow + translateY(-4px) over 180ms. The lift and shadow change happen together — never one without the other.

dos: Do use Ember Gold only for the active/primary state of one control per screen section.
Do add paper grain (editorial-paper-texture.webp, multiply blend, 3–7% opacity) to every card surface.
Do use the warm shadow tiers in order: Card at rest → Elevated on hover.
Do tighten heading tracking to −0.03em or lower at display/headline sizes.
Do keep label text ALL CAPS, 0.7rem, +0.08em, ink-muted.
Do use cinematic photography full-bleed in hero sections with directional dark overlay, ensuring ≥4.5:1 text contrast.
Do keep motion at 150–260ms for state changes, 700ms for scroll reveals.
Do collapse all --duration-* tokens to 1ms when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is active.
Do keep card borders as warm amber-tinted outlines at rest, deepening on hover.
donts: Don't use glassmorphism except on the hero controls card (a deliberate, isolated exception over a full-bleed photo).
Don't use neon, cyberpunk, or saturated blue/purple/green gradients.
Don't build generic SaaS UI — no filter bars, no star ratings, no Notion-clone whitespace.
Don't use cool or neutral grays. Every gray must be warm.
Don't add motion for its own sake — no bouncing loaders, no staggered-everything entrances.
Don't use pure black (#000000) anywhere. Warmest dark is #1c1917; cinematic dark is #0c1020.
Don't place text over a hero image without the directional overlay.
Don't put Ember Gold on more than 15% of any screen surface.
Don't use childish design patterns — no confetti, no gamification badges, no pastel blob illustrations.
